activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If it rains, we'll move the picnic indoors.
activities.explanation: La condición posible usa presente «rains» y la consecuencia futura usa «will move».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If it will rain, we'll move the picnic indoors.
activities.explanation: En una condición futura normal usamos presente después de «if»: «If it rains».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: You'll feel better if you get some rest.
activities.explanation: El orden invertido es correcto: «will» aparece en el resultado y presente en la condición.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If you don't hurry, you'll miss the beginning.
activities.explanation: La condición negativa usa presente «don't hurry» y el resultado «will miss».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If you won't hurry, you'll miss the beginning.
activities.explanation: Para una condición negativa normal corresponde «if you don't hurry», no «won't hurry».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the printer stops again, restart it from the control panel.
activities.explanation: La consecuencia puede ser un imperativo: «restart it».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the parcel arrives today, we might deliver it tomorrow.
activities.explanation: Un modal como «might» es válido para expresar un resultado posible, no seguro.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the parcel will arrive today, we might deliver it tomorrow.
activities.explanation: La cláusula condicional debe usar presente: «If the parcel arrives today».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: Unless you save the document, you'll lose your changes.
activities.explanation: «Unless you save» equivale a «if you don't save» y evita una negación adicional.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: Unless you don't save the document, you'll lose your changes.
activities.explanation: «Unless» ya significa «if not»; «unless you don't save» cambia el sentido y crea una doble negación no deseada.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the café is full, we can try the place next door.
activities.explanation: «Can» expresa una opción disponible como consecuencia de la condición.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the café is full we can try the place next door.
activities.explanation: Cuando la cláusula con «if» aparece primero, la convención escrita exige una coma tras «full».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: We won't be able to enter if we forget our passes.
activities.explanation: La principal negativa «won't be able to» precede correctamente a «if + presente».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If I finish the report early, I may join you for dinner.
activities.explanation: «May» expresa que la consecuencia sigue siendo posible aunque se cumpla la condición.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If I finished the report early, I will join you for dinner.
activities.explanation: La mezcla no es coherente para el primer condicional: debe ser «If I finish ..., I will ...».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If your symptoms get worse, you should seek medical advice.
activities.explanation: «Should» es adecuado para dar consejo como resultado de una condición futura posible.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: When the courier arrives, I'll sign for the package.
activities.explanation: «When» es correcto si el hablante espera la llegada; la cláusula temporal usa presente.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: When the courier will arrive, I'll sign for the package.
activities.explanation: Tras «when» temporal usamos presente: «When the courier arrives».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If we reduce the image size, the page should load faster.
activities.explanation: «Should» expresa un resultado esperado, pero no completamente seguro, de una condición viable.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: Provided that everyone agrees, we'll publish the schedule tomorrow.
activities.explanation: «Provided that» significa «con la condición de que» y admite presente seguido de una consecuencia futura.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: As long as the data remains private, we can use it for the study.
activities.explanation: «As long as» introduce una condición necesaria y combina correctamente presente con «can».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: Even if the price falls, we won't replace equipment that still works.
activities.explanation: «Even if» indica que el resultado no cambiará; la estructura presente más «won't» es correcta.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the figures will be accurate, the committee will approve the budget.
activities.explanation: La condición normal debe usar presente: «If the figures are accurate».

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If the figures are accurate, the committee is likely to approve the budget.
activities.explanation: El resultado no tiene que llevar «will»; «is likely to approve» expresa una consecuencia probable.

activities.instructions: Decide whether the first conditional sentence is correct.
activities.prompt: If you'll wait in reception, the director will see you shortly.
activities.explanation: Aquí «will» tras «if» expresa disposición cortés a esperar, no simple futuro; es una excepción válida.
